evals.prompt: I can't connect to the .NET API from my Next.js app locally — not sure what to put in .env.local.
evals.expected_output: Finds the Next.js project, asks what URL the local .NET API runs on (suggesting launchSettings.json as a source), and writes API_BASE_URL to .env.local rather than .env.
expectations: Locates the Next.js project via package.json + next.config
Asks the user for the local .NET API's URL rather than guessing one
Mentions launchSettings.json (or running the API) as a way to find the URL if the user doesn't know it
Reminds the user to restart the dev server after writing the value

evals.prompt: After setting API_BASE_URL to https://localhost:5001, I'm getting a self-signed certificate error when Next.js calls the API.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as the .NET dev HTTPS certificate, and offers NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 as a local-only workaround without suggesting it for anything beyond a developer machine.
expectations: Identifies the self-signed dev certificate as the likely cause
Suggests NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 as a local-only workaround
Explicitly warns this is not appropriate beyond a local dev machine (not for production/shared environments)
